content.problemStatement:
You are given two arrays AAA and BBB, of lengths NNN and MMM respectively. You are also given an empty array CCC.
In one operation you can:


Append the entirety of AAA to CCC; or
Increase any element of CCC by 111.
Find the minimum number of operations needed such that BBB becomes a subsequence of CCC or print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible to make BBB as a subsequence of CCC after some number of operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the lengths of arrays AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1A_1A1‚Äč,A2A_2A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN\ldots , A_N‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of array AAA.
The third line contains MMM space-separated integers B1B_1B1‚Äč,B2B_2B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BM\ldots ,B_M‚Ä¶,BM‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the elements of array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations needed such that BBB becomes a subsequence of CCC or print ‚ąí1-1‚ąí1 if it is impossible to make BBB as a subsequence of CCC after some number of operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: One sequence of operations is as follows.

Initially, C=[]C = []C=[]. Append AAA to it, to obtain [5,2][5, 2][5,2].
Append AAA to it again, to obtain C=[5,2,5,2]C = [5, 2 ,5 ,2]C=[5,2,5,2].
Increase C2C_2C2‚Äč twice and C4C_4C4‚Äč once, to obtain [5,4,5,3][5, 4, 5, 3][5,4,5,3].
This has B=[4,5,3]B = [4, 5, 3]B=[4,5,3] as a subsequence, as required. We used 555 moves in total.

Test case 222: Append AAA to CCC four times to obtain [10,10,10,10][10, 10, 10, 10][10,10,10,10], then increase the elements to obtain C=[11,15,14,10]C = [11, 15, 14, 10]C=[11,15,14,10]. This requires 141414 moves in total.
Test case 333: It can be shown that no matter what, BBB cannot appear as a subsequence of CCC.
Test case 444: Append AAA to CCC once. BBB is already a subsequence of AAA, so this is enough.
